identificationModule.briefTitle: The NOrdic-Baltic Randomized Registry Study for Evaluation of PCI in Chronic Total Coronary Occlusion
identificationModule.officialTitle: The NOrdic-Baltic Randomized Registry Study for Long-term Clinical Evaluation of Adjunction of PCI to Optimal Medical Therapy in Chronic Total Coronary Occlusion

descriptionModule.briefSummary: Randomized registry for the study of CTO PCI as adjunction to optimal medical therapy.

conditions: Chronic Total Occlusion of Coronary Artery

designInfo.interventionModelDescription: Randomized registry with option of crossover after 6months

armGroups.label: Initial conservative treatment
armGroups.description: Optimal medical therapy and option for crossover after 6 months or fulfillment of certain conditions

armGroups.label: initial interventional treatment
armGroups.description: CTO PCI attempt as initial strategy with medical optimization simultaneously

interventions.name: CTO PCI
interventions.description: attempted percutaneous coronary intervention of the chronic total coronary occlusion

primaryOutcomes.description: All-cause mortality after inclusion of 2000 patients
Quality of life assessment by 12-Item Short Form Survey Instrument (SF-12v2)

secondaryOutcomes.description: Reduction of inducible myocardial perfusion defect evaluated, improvement of left ventricular function and correlation of angina and perfusion defect
evaluation of systolic and diastolic left ventricular function compared to index echocardiography before CTO PCI.

otherOutcomes.description: Socio-economic consequenses of CTO PCI. Treatment cost compared to coronary bypass surgery

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* ≥1 CTO lesion amenable to PCI.
* Stable and stabilized coronary artery disease
* Symptoms (angina pectoris or shortness of breath) and/or signs of reversible perfusion defect by SPECT, PET or MR and/or angiographic/echocardiographic indication of reversible ischemia.
* CTO lesion in a major coronary vessel supplying a significant myocardial territory (vessel diameter usually ≥3mm).

Exclusion Criteria:

* Expected survival \<1 year.
* Renal failure on dialysis.
* Stable non-CTO lesions treated within one month.
* Declined informed consent.
* Regarding CMR: allergy to contrast medium, severe obesity, claustrophobia and certain metallic implants

references.citation: Christensen MK, Eftekhari A, Raungaard B, Steigen TK, Kumsaars I, Riahi S, Sogaard P, Thuesen L. Impact of Percutaneous Intervention Compared to Pharmaceutical Therapy on Complex Arrhythmias in Patients With Chronic Total Coronary Occlusion. Rationale and Design of the CTO-ARRHYTHMIA Study. Cardiovasc Revasc Med. 2023 Sep;54:69-72. doi: 10.1016/j.carrev.2023.04.001. Epub 2023 Apr 10.
Rinfret S, Sandesara PB. Reducing Ischemia With CTO PCI: Good News, But Questions Remain. JACC Cardiovasc Interv. 2021 Jul 12;14(13):1419-1422. doi: 10.1016/j.jcin.2021.05.028. No abstract available.
